Title: SbC1, an R2R3-MYB transcription factor, specifically regulates anthocyanin accumulation in sorghum coleoptiles

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; subgroup 5 MYB; contains canonical bHLH-binding motif [D/E]Lx2[R/K]x3Lx6Lx3R
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: HYZ and QKY (sorghum); 9311 (rice OsC1 mutant background)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Sorghum coleoptiles; in rice overexpression lines also leaf sheath, ligule, stigma, apiculus, and hull apex
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protects seedlings and contributes to abiotic stress tolerance, especially drought resistance
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes including CHS; CHI; F3H; F3'H; DFR; ANS; UFGT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively upregulates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, with especially strong effects on F3H, DFR, ANS, and UFGT
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation specifically in sorghum coleoptiles; complementation in rice restores anthocyanin pigmentation and improves drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator specifically governing anthocyanin biosynthesis in sorghum coleoptiles
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with Tan1, suggesting participation in an anthocyanin-related MBW-type regulatory complex; no interaction detected with Tan2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in stem/coleoptile tissues at the five-leaf stage in sorghum cultivar HYZ
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in sorghum coleoptiles; transgene expression in rice under constitutive promoter causes pigmentation in multiple vegetative and floral tissues
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize ubiquitin promoter (UBI, constitutive) for rice transformation described in Methods; 35S promoter stated in Results for rice complementation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought simulation by 20% PEG-6000; seedling-stage coleoptile pigmentation assessment at 7–10 days after germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; Sanger validation of causal SNP; VIGS knockdown; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; Y2H; BiFC; Split-LUC; Co-IP; heterologous overexpression/complementation in rice; anthocyanin quantification; drought assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing/SNP data from 242 and 479 sorghum accessions; raw sequence data deposited in China National GeneBank CNP0002968 and European Variant Archive PRJEB51985; RNA-seq on HYZ vs QKY coleoptiles (accession not provided in text)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive overexpression in rice causes broad ectopic pigmentation; no significant growth difference under normal irrigation was reported, but possible later-stage field effects remain untested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful allele for breeding or engineering anthocyanin-rich coleoptiles and improving drought tolerance in sorghum and other cereals; allele selection or transgenic/editing deployment should consider tissue specificity and promoter choice
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural loss-of-function allele sbc1-a carries a C-to-T mutation at the 124th base of exon 1, converting Gln codon CAG to premature stop codon TAG; this allele specifically affects coleoptile anthocyanin but not grain pigmentation. SbC1 is absent/misannotated in older BTx623 v3.1.1 reference and was identified via comparison with HYZ genome.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and comparative genomics identified SbC1 as the candidate gene at the coleoptile color locus on sorghum chromosome 10. 2. A natural nonsense mutation (sbc1-a) abolishes SbC1 function and is perfectly associated with green coleoptiles. 3. VIGS knockdown of SbC1 decreases its transcript level and sharply reduces anthocyanin content in coleoptiles. 4. Transcriptome and qRT-PCR analyses show that SbC1 activates multiple anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, especially F3H, DFR, ANS, and UFGT. 5. SbC1 localizes to the nucleus and physically interacts with Tan1. 6. Heterologous expression of SbC1 rescues anthocyanin deficiency in rice OsC1 mutant 9311 and improves drought tolerance.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40 protein; sorghum homolog of Arabidopsis TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: HYZ/QKY context; interaction tested in tobacco transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: anthocyanin biosynthesis regulation; previously also linked to grain condensed tannin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein interaction/scaffold function in regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely supports SbC1-mediated activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes through protein interaction, but direct target genes were not shown
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May participate in regulating anthocyanin accumulation in sorghum coleoptiles through interaction with SbC1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally supportive regulator; interacts with the decisive regulator SbC1 but its direct causal role in coleoptile anthocyanin accumulation was not functionally proven here
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SbC1; Tan2 (positive control interaction reported in Y2H context from prior work)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Candidate component of an MBW-like complex with SbC1; direct interaction with SbC1 supported by Y2H, BiFC, Split-LUC, and Co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (interaction with SbC1 observed in nucleus)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Relatively high expression in sorghum coleoptiles
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Relatively high in coleoptiles according to expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; Split-LUC; Co-IP; tissue expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional variation in prior studies affects grain anthocyanin/tannin biosynthesis but not coleoptile anthocyanin biosynthesis, implying tissue-specific roles
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-regulator to consider when engineering anthocyanin traits, but redundancy and tissue specificity require clarification before deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper did not directly manipulate Tan1 in sorghum coleoptiles, so evidence supports interaction and possible participation rather than definitive causal control. Discussion notes possible redundancy of WD40 proteins in coleoptiles.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tan1 is a WD40 regulator homologous to TTG1 and is relatively highly expressed in sorghum coleoptiles. 2. Tan1 physically interacts with SbC1 in yeast, tobacco nucleus, and Co-IP assays. 3. These data suggest Tan1 may participate in coleoptile anthocyanin regulation with SbC1, but direct functional proof in coleoptiles was not established in this study.
